target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the CLI run and request-log assertion:

```text
[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] startup stages: runId=c43c486b-23d0-4e83-8fd1-c0dd12f67cba sessionId=proof-subagent-session phase=attempt-dispatch totalMs=5818 stages=workspace:1ms@1ms,runtime-plugins:5605ms@5606ms,hooks:6ms@5612ms,model-resolution:113ms@5725ms,auth:67ms@5792ms,context-engine:1ms@5793ms,attempt-workspace:1ms@5794ms,attempt-prompt:0ms@5794ms,attempt-runtime-plan:24ms@5818ms,attempt-dispatch:0ms@5818ms
```

```json
{
  "head": "25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": ["exec", "read"],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): isolate provider catalog projection failures
target.body:
## Summary

- isolates provider catalog projection so unreadable plugin-owned provider/model rows are skipped instead of hiding healthy siblings
- filters malformed provider auth entries before provider registration while preserving valid auth methods and env vars
- guards paired provider catalog API-key injection from throwing provider configs

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed 1 shard / 14 tests after final rebase
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts src/shared/safe-record.ts` passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts src/shared/safe-record.ts` passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed after final rebase
- private reported-name scrub over the branch diff passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed clean, overall 0.84
- AWS Crabbox changed gate passed: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_508d1fb17e4f`, run `run_421055b8dbcb`, slug `harbor-crab`, command `pnpm check:changed`, selected lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, exit 0
- Blacksmith Testbox proof was attempted first but local Blacksmith auth is missing; AWS Crabbox was used instead of running the broad gate locally

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: hostile or malformed plugin provider catalog/auth rows no longer make provider projection fail before healthy sibling provider data can be used.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw gwt worktree rebased onto current `origin/main`, final head `1fcab057102b`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebased the pruned provider-catalog slice onto current `origin/main`, reran focused provider-entry/provider-catalog tests, formatter, lint, diff check, private-name scrub, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: focused regressions pass for unreadable provider catalog entries, unreadable provider model rows, unreadable provider auth option rows, and unreadable paired provider catalog configs while preserving healthy siblings.

Observed result after fix: healthy provider catalog rows and auth methods remain registered/projected while only unreadable sibling rows are omitted.

What was not tested: full repo-wide test suite or a live external plugin process; this PR uses the existing provider/plugin SDK harness plus remote changed-gate proof to exercise the projection paths directly.

target.title: fix(agents): count streamed model deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary
- Count streamed `text_delta`/`thinking_delta`/`toolcall_delta` diagnostics from their incremental `delta` bytes instead of serializing the full event.
- Drop growing `partial` snapshots from fallback stream byte accounting so diagnostics do not repeatedly walk the accumulated assistant message.
- Add regression coverage proving text-delta accounting does not serialize large partial snapshots and keeps streams alive if byte inspection throws.
- Document the bounded `responseStreamBytes` semantics for high-frequency streamed deltas and align OTEL docs/metric descriptions with that bounded payload contract.

Refs #86599.

## Verification
- Rebasing onto current `origin/main` completed on head `c1783a4997e`.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md docs/logging.md`: passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md docs/logging.md extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts`: passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts`: passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`: passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts --reporter=dot`: passed, 2 shards / 86 tests.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: passed, `provider=aws`, `lease=cbx_fc87d734de03`, `run=run_dafa0367419e`, exit 0; selected lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, `docs`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: dense model streams no longer make model-call diagnostics serialize the full growing `partial` assistant message on every delta.

Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a linked OpenClaw gwt worktree, plus AWS Crabbox Linux changed-gate proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts --reporter=dot`; docs mdx, oxfmt, oxlint, diff-check; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label diagnostic-delta-accounting-check-changed --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: focused tests passed, including assertions that `text_delta` accounting does not call `partial.toJSON`, counts incremental `delta` bytes, and keeps stream delivery alive when byte inspection cannot read a chunk. AWS `check:changed` passed the selected core, core test, extension, extension test, and docs lanes.

Observed result after fix: `responseStreamBytes` is computed from incremental delta bytes for high-frequency stream deltas and from snapshotless chunks for other partial-bearing stream events, while OTEL metadata and docs describe the exported histogram as bounded streamed response payload bytes.

What was not tested: a live long-running local-model session.

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex produced the implementation and PR shepherding with OpenAI `gpt-5.5`; ragesaq reviewed the direction and required production-grade proof before merge.

Pass a typed `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract through the context-engine lifecycle for embedded, CLI, and Codex app-server runs. Context engines now receive a versioned snapshot of runtime mode, model/provider state, host capabilities, token limits, and diagnostics instead of only the assemble-time `model` string.

## Changes

- Add `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` and `ContextEngineRuntimeMode` in `src/context-engine/types.ts`.
- Add `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Thread `runtimeSettings` through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, compact, and maintenance paths.
- Export the runtime-settings contract and errors through the public plugin SDK barrel.
- Preserve strict legacy context engines: if an engine rejects `runtimeSettings` as an unknown key, OpenClaw retries the lifecycle call without it instead of quarantining the engine.
- Document the public context-engine runtime-settings contract in `docs/concepts/context-engine.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`.
- Update the plugin SDK export contract test for the new public runtime-settings error exports.
- Add configured-slot proof that a resolved context engine receives runtimeSettings across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact.
- Preserve the same overflow compaction runtimeSettings into the immediately-following post-compaction maintenance call.

## Current Head

Current PR head: `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`.

Scope cleanup: the unrelated Copilot Gemini model-id commit `747f09e19610` has been removed from this PR by rewriting the branch over the current `release/2026.5.31` base. The current PR stack is 8 context-engine/runtime-settings commits, 20 files changed, and no `extensions/github-copilot` files in the diff.

## Review Findings Addressed

- [P1] Strict-schema legacy engines could quarantine on the new `runtimeSettings` key. Fixed by adding `runtimeSettings` to the registry legacy strip/retry set for bootstrap, maintain, afterTurn, assemble, and compact. Covered by focused compatibility tests. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P1] Current-head configured-plugin proof was missing. Added `passes runtime settings through a configured context engine across lifecycle hooks`, which registers an engine, resolves it through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, and verifies runtimeSettings on bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact. Commit: `9de0196a4b`.
- [P2] Overflow compaction built runtimeSettings for `compact()` but dropped them on immediate post-compaction maintenance. Fixed by reusing the same runtimeSettings object for both calls and covering the handoff in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`. Commit: `a767005b61`.
- [P2] `model.family` could mirror a concrete model id. Fixed by defaulting family to `null` unless a real family is supplied. Covered by regression tests. Commits: `2f1c92fc28`, `08ae320eca`.
- [P2] SDK contract was exported but not documented. Fixed by documenting `runtimeSettings` in context-engine docs and SDK overview. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P2] `compact()` documented `runtimeSettings`, but real compact callers did not pass it. Fixed embedded timeout/overflow compaction, queued/manual embedded compaction, CLI compaction, and follow-up maintenance to pass typed runtime settings. Commits: `b15732c179`, `a767005b61`.
- [P2] Production embedded paths did not populate requested/resolved/fallback/degraded fields. Fixed embedded attempts to capture requested model and pass fallback metadata through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, and maintenance. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- CI failure in `checks-fast-contracts-plugins-b`: fixed `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` to include `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnavailableError` and `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- Copilot review: `fallbackReason` and `fallbackActive` could be inconsistent. Fixed by deriving `fallbackActive` from any non-empty fallback reason. Covered by regression test. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- Copilot review: runtime-settings Error subclasses lacked names. Fixed. Commit: `2f1c92fc28`.
- Copilot review: missing builder test branches for degraded mode and invalid capabilities. Fixed. Commit: `14e1385788`.

## Current-Head Proof

Environment: OpenClaw source tree for this PR branch, run locally against current head `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`. Proof output is sanitized: no home paths, tokens, private endpoints, or raw transcripts are included here.

Commands run at current head:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm build
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1
```

Observed result:

```text
plugin-sdk-index.test.ts: 4 passed
context-engine.test.ts: 42 passed
runtime-settings.test.ts: 6 passed
context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts: 12 passed
run.overflow-compaction.test.ts: included in focused agent shard; focused agent shard 52 passed
pnpm check:test-types: exit 0
pnpm build: exit 0, including check-plugin-sdk-exports OK
git diff --check: clean
contracts-plugin config: 64 files, 894 tests passed
```

Configured context-engine proof added in `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- registers a test context engine with bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintain, and compact hooks
- resolves that engine through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`
- drives the resolved engine through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn/finalization maintenance, and compact timeout wrapper
- asserts every captured hook receives schema version 1 runtimeSettings
- asserts fallback mode, requested/resolved model split, provider, `family: null`, `fallbackActive: true`, and `fallbackReason`

Overflow compaction proof added in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`:

- drives overflow recovery through the embedded runner harness
- asserts the same runtimeSettings object reaches engine-owned overflow `compact()` and follow-up `runContextEngineMaintenance()`
- asserts post-compaction maintenance sees degraded mode and `degradedReason: "context_overflow"`

Compatibility proof added in `context-engine.test.ts`:

- strict legacy `assemble()` retries without `sessionKey`, `prompt`, and `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `bootstrap()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `maintain()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `afterTurn()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `compact()` retries without `runtimeSettings`

Runtime-settings proof added in `runtime-settings.test.ts` and `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- `model.family` defaults to `null` and never mirrors a concrete model id
- explicit `modelFamily` is preserved when supplied
- `fallbackReason` forces `fallbackActive: true` and `runtime.mode: "fallback"`
- `degradedReason` yields `runtime.mode: "degraded"`
- invalid host capabilities fail closed with `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`

## Compatibility and Risk

`runtimeSettings` remains optional on every lifecycle hook param. Engines that ignore it continue unchanged. Engines with strict unknown-field validation now get the same compatibility retry behavior OpenClaw already uses for legacy `sessionKey` and `prompt` additions.

Remaining review risk: current-head live Gateway proof is still a maintainer policy question. The branch now has current-head source-level configured-engine proof and current-head focused/full local validation, and it no longer contains the unrelated provider-default change.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts` -> pass, focused shards including 52 agent tests.
- `pnpm check:test-types` -> pass.
- `pnpm build` -> pass.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` -> pass, 894 tests total.

## Operator Understanding

ragesaq asked for proof from actual use before merge, called out that labels still required proof/triage proof, asked for PR follow-up and cleanup, then asked to clean up the unrelated Gemini commit. The PR has been rewritten onto the current release base so the public diff is now scoped to runtime settings only.

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- A rolling timer (20s cadence) shows elapsed time + a wall-clock stamp while running, so a stalled lane is visible at a glance; cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane. Tagged payloads (`<think>reason</think>answer`) keep only the in-tag reasoning, so answer prose after `</think>` never lands in the lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **Rate-limit-aware cadence** — the rolling "still running" timer ticks every 20s (not every few seconds) so N concurrently-streaming Thinking messages stay under Telegram's per-chat edit rate and don't draw 429s (a 429 drops the edit and stalls the lane); the line carries a wall-clock stamp so a genuine stall is obvious
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Prerequisite

- **#80046** (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args) — **merged 2026-05-29**; supplies the `onToolStart` lifecycle events the tool lines render. This branch should be rebased onto current `main` (which now contains it) before merge.

The renderer otherwise consumes only existing callbacks and is default-off, so it depends on no unmerged work. (On the standard `claude-cli` backend, CLI-*native* reasoning text additionally benefits from #85381's `thinking_delta`; without it the lane simply shows tool/event lines. The interactive backend restores thinking via its proxy, so reasoning appears regardless. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected — this is not a hard dependency.)

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard + wall-clock timer stamp, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append, timer-interval constant)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; 20s rolling-timer cadence; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (extract only in-`<think>` reasoning, dropping answer prose after `</think>`, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle + wall-clock stamp, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, tagged-reasoning split (answer after `</think>` dropped; streaming/code cases), defensive final-answer strip
- [x] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Verification

Local checks at the current head:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions                                                 # typecheck — clean
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts   # lint — clean
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts \
               extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.test.ts   # all green
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opt-in Telegram interleaved progress renders reasoning plus tool/progress events in one durable Thinking message, without duplicating the final answer or freezing at Telegram message limits.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw Telegram gateway against Telegram, default-off feature explicitly enabled, two concurrent Telegram threads in one chat.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt and ran the gateway from the PR head, enabled channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress, sent concurrent Telegram prompts that produced reasoning, tool progress, long Thinking output, and idle tool waits, then inspected Telegram messages and the gateway outbound Telegram send log.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot embedded below plus redacted outbound Telegram send-log metrics copied below: continuation msg=34783 -> msg=34786, final answer absent from every Thinking edit, zero bare Thinking sends, 20s timer edits at 04:20:15 -> 04:20:35 -> 04:20:55, and 99 outbound calls with 0 failures.

Observed result after fix: Thinking lane showed reasoning plus tool line, rolled into a continuation before the cap, sent the polished answer once as its own message, cleared empty Thinking output, and avoided Telegram 429s under the two-thread concurrent run.

What was not tested: No broad channel matrix or release package lane in this PR; focused local proof covers the Telegram renderer helpers, schema, lint, and extension typecheck.

## Live proof (current head)

Captured on the current head with the default-off feature enabled, over a **two-thread concurrent** session. The screenshot shows the visible lane; the rest is verified from the gateway's **outbound Telegram send log** — every `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` the bot issues — which shows message-level behavior a screenshot can't (message ids, edit cadence, final-state content, timing). Wire-level facts below are message-level metrics (ids, lengths, ok/timing) and contain no command/path/host content.

**Normal interleaving — reasoning + tool-use line in the Thinking lane, with the rolling timer:**

![interleaved lane](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/66da4d16b5a9467a82a65134976bc66aa956519c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-30-toolline.png)

**Wire-level verification (gateway send log, current head, one two-thread concurrent session):**

- **Long-message continuation** — `msg=34783` grew to the 4000-char cap and rolled into a fresh continuation `msg=34786` (carrying the next tool line) instead of freezing; clean seam, no garble.
- **No duplicate final answer** — on that long thinking-heavy turn the final answer was delivered once as its own message and is **absent from every Thinking edit** of `34783`/`34786`.
- **No empty Thinking** — **zero** bare-`Thinking`-header sends across the session.
- **20s rolling-timer cadence (rate-limit relief)** — while idling on a blocking tool, the "still running" line re-edited at **exactly 20s intervals** (04:20:15 → 04:20:35 → 04:20:55, body static at 3764 chars), keeping concurrent Thinking messages under Telegram's edit rate. The wall-clock stamp exposes any genuine stall.
- **No rate-limiting** — **99** outbound calls across the two concurrent threads, **0** failures (no `429`s, no oversized-preview stop) — the exact concurrency that previously drew 429s.

target.title: fix(gemini): bridge OAuth profiles into CLI runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- opt `google-gemini-cli` into explicit OpenClaw auth-profile forwarding
- refresh/load the selected OAuth profile before CLI backend preparation
- materialize selected Gemini OAuth credentials into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` for the `gemini` subprocess
- keep Gemini CLI auth epochs profile-owned when the bridge is active

Fixes #88742.

## Proof

HEAD: `d884722e235342d016211e82bc4085d228261259`

Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-validation.log`

Commands:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
pnpm -s exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Relevant output:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  59 passed (59)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Source-level behavior proof: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-proof.log`

- `extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts` proves selected OAuth credentials produce an isolated Gemini CLI home and writes `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` without logging token material.
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` proves canonical `google/*` models routed through `google-gemini-cli` select the `google-gemini-cli:*` auth profile before `runCliAgent`.

Not tested:

- Full `pnpm -s exec tsgo --noEmit` is blocked on current HEAD by pre-existing errors outside this patch:

```text
src/config/io.ts(192,7): error TS2527: The inferred type of 'configWritePostCommitRollback' references an inaccessible 'unique symbol' type. A type annotation is necessary.
src/secrets/config-io.ts(8,17): error TS4058: Return type of exported function has or is using name 'configWritePostCommitRollback' from external module ".../src/config/io" but cannot be named.
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `google-gemini-cli` backend preparation receives a selected OpenClaw `google-gemini-cli:*` OAuth profile, materializes that OAuth profile into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`, and passes that isolated home to the real `gemini` subprocess instead of relying on ambient `~/.gemini` state.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Linux workstation, PR branch `fix/88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge`, head `d884722e235342d016211e82bc4085d228261259`, OpenClaw `2026.5.31 (7150977)`, `@google/gemini-cli` binary available as `/usr/local/bin/gemini`, real Gemini CLI version `0.40.1`. Token values and account identifiers were not printed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
cd /home/rev/projects/openclaw-prs/pr-88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge
pnpm install
pnpm build
confirmed backend registration path:
  rg -n 'buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend|prepareExecution|CliBackendPlugin|cliBackends' src packages extensions/google | head -120
  nl -ba extensions/google/cli-backend.ts | sed -n '1,155p'
created direct backend proof harness:
  .tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts
ran:
  BAD_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy PROFILE='google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com' pnpm exec tsx ./.tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log
sanitized:
  sed -E -e 's/ya29\.[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-access-token>/g' -e 's#1//[A-Za-z0-9._-]+#<redacted-refresh-token>#g' -e 's/eyJ[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-jwt>/g' -e 's/AIza[[:alnum:]_-]+/<redacted-google-api-key>/g' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log > /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
verified no obvious token material remained:
  grep -E 'ya29\.|1//|eyJ|AIza' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
=== PR 88748 direct Gemini CLI backend proof ===
backend_id=google-gemini-cli
profile=google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com
bad_parent_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy
prepared_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB
prepared_home_is_bad_parent=false
home_mode=700
gemini_dir_mode=700
oauth_creds_path=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB/.gemini/oauth_creds.json
oauth_creds_mode=600
oauth_creds_shape={"has_access_token":true,"has_refresh_token":true,"has_expiry_date":true,"token_type":"Bearer","has_id_token":false}
real_gemini_exit=0
real_gemini_stdout=0.40.1
real_gemini_stderr=
cleanup_removed_home=true
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend().prepareExecution(...)` accepted a `google-gemini-cli` OAuth profile, created a private isolated Gemini CLI home, wrote `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` with Bearer OAuth credential shape and restrictive file permissions, returned a `GEMINI_CLI_HOME` different from the deliberately bad parent home, and a real `gemini` subprocess started successfully with that prepared environment. Cleanup removed the isolated home afterward.
- **What was not tested**: The proof intentionally did not print raw Google OAuth access tokens, refresh tokens, ID tokens, or private account data. The subprocess proof used `gemini --version` to prove the real Gemini CLI binary starts under the prepared `GEMINI_CLI_HOME`.

target.title: fix(cli): show Gemini CLI runtime auth status
target.body:
## Summary
- include CLI runtime auth providers in `models status` runtime-auth accounting
- report canonical `google/*` text/default routes using `google-gemini-cli` as runtime auth routes while keeping image routes on Google API-key auth
- update Gemini CLI OAuth setup to write the existing provider/model-scoped `agentRuntime` policy on the default Google model instead of the ignored whole-agent runtime key
- keep CLI runtime auth checks provider-specific so `claude-cli` is not considered usable from canonical Anthropic auth/env that the CLI runtime clears
- add regression coverage for `GEMINI_API_KEY` plus Gemini CLI OAuth profile, login-generated config patching, plugin-owned CLI runtime synthetic auth, and missing native CLI runtime auth

Fixes #79585.

## Surface area
- No public config schema changes.
- No plugin API or plugin contract changes.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or setup surfaces.
- This only changes `models status` auth reporting/checking and the existing Gemini CLI OAuth setup patch target.

## Refresh note
Rebased onto `main` `be1273182eb971f0bdfcdd807ab7b0939e4177ca`.

Signed head: `b1fa3e731609fbb5ab5d30b9ec8dbded7c963143`.

## Follow-up fixes after review
- Fixed `no-shadow` by renaming the local runtime-resolution variable.
- Added CLI runtime providers to synthetic-auth probing, so plugin-owned runtime auth such as `claude-cli` is detected for runtime routes.
- Kept runtime-route checks direct/provider-specific, so canonical Anthropic auth or env does not make a `claude-cli` route pass when native Claude CLI auth is missing.
- Added regressions for both cases:
  - `anthropic/*` routed through `claude-cli` with plugin-owned synthetic auth is usable.
  - `anthropic/*` routed through `claude-cli` with only canonical Anthropic auth reports missing `claude-cli` and fails `--check`.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 39 tests passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/gemini-cli-provider.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts` -> passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/gemini-cli-provider.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
- `/home/giodl/.local/toolchains/node-v22.22.3-linux-x64/bin/codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable correctness issues found
- `git log --show-signature --format="%H %G? %s" origin/main..HEAD` -> all 4 commits have good signatures

## Remaining proof gap
I still have not run live Gemini CLI OAuth login plus `models status --check` on this machine. The branch now has the static/focused coverage and Codex review clean, but the ClawSweeper live-proof request remains unless a maintainer accepts the remaining risk or live Gemini CLI OAuth proof is produced.

No merge performed.

target.title: fix(codex): quarantine unreadable dynamic tools
target.body:
## Summary

- quarantines Codex dynamic tools with unreadable entries, unreadable required fields, invalid names, reserved MCP names, or unsupported input schemas before Codex registration
- preserves healthy sibling tools so a bad plugin-owned dynamic tool cannot poison the whole assistant turn before content
- emits warning and diagnostic telemetry for quarantined tools while denying calls to quarantined tools in-band

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed 1 shard / 45 tests after final rebase
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed after final rebase
- final-file and added-line private reported-name scrubs passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed clean, overall 0.84
- AWS Crabbox changed gate was attempted twice after rebase but did not allocate because the coordinator active lease limit was exceeded (`9/8`); no remote command executed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: one malformed plugin-owned dynamic tool no longer prevents Codex from registering healthy dynamic tools and starting the assistant turn.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw gwt worktree rebased onto current `origin/main`, final head `38e523b232e`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebased the Codex dynamic-tool quarantine slice onto current `origin/main`, reran focused Codex dynamic-tool tests, formatter, lint, diff check, private-name scrub, branch autoreview, and attempted AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: focused regressions pass for unsupported schemas, unreadable tool array entries, unreadable `parameters`, unreadable `description`, empty names, whitespace names, invalid-character names, too-long names, and reserved MCP names while preserving the healthy `message` sibling.

Observed result after fix: invalid dynamic tools are omitted from Codex specs, recorded in telemetry, logged/diagnosed as quarantined, and their calls return an in-band unknown/unavailable-tool response instead of crashing registration.

What was not tested: remote `pnpm check:changed` after this rebase, because both AWS Crabbox allocation attempts were rejected by the active lease cap and Blacksmith Testbox auth is unavailable in this environment.

target.title: fix(mcp): reject mixed command+url transport config (#72111)
target.body:
Closes #72111.

## Problem

When an MCP server config carries **both** a non-empty `command` and a non-empty `url` (e.g. a streamable-http endpoint plus a `command`/`args` pair), the runtime silently picks stdio and drops the HTTP transport. The user sees:

```
MCP server connection timed out after 30000ms
```

The spawned child never speaks JSON-RPC over stdio because the user actually wanted the streamable-http endpoint. The clawsweeper bot triaged this and recommended early rejection over silent fallback.

## Root cause

`resolveMcpTransportConfig` in `src/agents/mcp-transport-config.ts` resolves the stdio launch first when `command` is set, regardless of whether `url` is also set. The 30s timeout that follows is the user-visible symptom; the silent fallback at config-resolution time is the real bug.

## Reproducer

In `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`:

```json
{
  \"mcp\": {
    \"servers\": {
      \"broken\": {
        \"command\": \"node\",
        \"args\": [\"something.js\"],
        \"url\": \"https://my-mcp.example.com/mcp\"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Run OpenClaw → the `broken` server hangs, then errors with `MCP server connection timed out after 30000ms`. There is no clue in the logs about *which* transport was actually attempted.

## Fix

Reject the ambiguous shape early with a clear diagnostic that names the offending server and the requested transport hint, matching the file's existing \"skipped server X because Y\" diagnostic style and the clawsweeper bot's \"Best possible solution\":

> reject mixed `command` + `url`/HTTP transport configs early with a clear diagnostic and docs/test alignment. The current silent stdio precedence should not remain the user-visible timeout path.

Implementation: when `command` and a non-whitespace `url` are both present, log the warning, return `null` (caller already handles `null` as \"skip this server\"), and let the user fix their config.

Pure stdio configs (no `url`) and pure HTTP configs (no `command`) are unaffected. **Whitespace-only `url`** strings continue to be treated as \"no url\" so stdio still wins, matching the existing trimming in `mcp-stdio.ts`.

Diff: **+17** lines in `mcp-transport-config.ts`, **+52** in tests.

## Verification

```
pnpm vitest run src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts
# 11 passed (7 original + 4 new)

pnpm check
# 0 errors, 0 warnings; all policy guards green
```

## Tests added (4 new)

1. **Mixed config + transport hint** → resolution returns `null`; warning lists both `command` and `url` and the requested transport.
2. **Mixed config without explicit transport hint** → still rejected; warning is informative.
3. **Whitespace-only `url` paired with real `command`** → resolution returns stdio (mirrors `mcp-stdio.ts` trimming).
4. **Sanitized transport in warning** → diagnostic does not leak full URL secrets / query strings.

## Risk notes

- **Behavior change is user-visible**: configs that used to silently fall back to stdio when both `command`+`url` were set now get rejected outright (server skipped, no spawn). That is the bot-blessed shape and matches the issue, but anyone relying on the silent fallback (unlikely — it was the bug) will see a behavior shift on upgrade.
- Did NOT touch `src/agents/mcp-stdio.ts` even though the bot's evidence cites it; the rejection is cleaner at the transport-config layer where both fields are visible together.
- PR collision check: 8 open MCP-related PRs in the repo (#66542, #69417, #69039, #65574, #62134, #64316, #62160, #60005); none touch `mcp-transport-config.ts` validation logic for the mixed shape. Safe to land independently.

target.title: refactor(whatsapp): introduce inbound message contexts
target.body:
## Summary

This PR reorganizes WhatsApp `WebInboundMessage` so inbound callback data is grouped by what the data represents instead of being exposed only as one large flat object.

The new shape separates inbound message facts into five explicit contexts:

- `event`: message id, timestamp, and batching metadata
- `payload`: message body, media, location, and structured payload context
- `quote`: quoted/replied-to message context
- `group`: group subject, participants, and mention metadata
- `platform`: WhatsApp transport details and actions such as reply, media send, and typing

Downstream WhatsApp auto-reply, monitor, identity, group gating, reaction, reply delivery, media, quote, and test-support code now reads these facts from the new contexts. The shipped flat callback fields and methods remain available as deprecated aliases so existing callback consumers can upgrade without a breaking shape change.

## API Shape

Code that receives a WhatsApp inbound message should prefer the relevant context, for example `msg.payload.body`, `msg.event.id`, `msg.group?.mentions`, `msg.quote?.body`, and `msg.platform.reply(...)`.

For compatibility, existing flat callback fields and methods such as `msg.body`, `msg.chatId`, `msg.reply(...)`, and `msg.mediaPath` are still populated and marked deprecated in the exported `WebInboundMessage` type.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Review Note

The inbound callback contract now exposes the nested context shape while preserving the shipped flat callback aliases. The runtime only creates optional contexts when their facts exist, so non-media messages do not expose a truthy `payload.media` and direct debounced batches do not expose a truthy `group` context.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp inbound callback messages now expose event, payload, quote, group, and platform contexts while preserving deprecated flat aliases for the shipped callback contract.

Real environment tested: Local focused WhatsApp extension tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The commands listed in Verification were run after the final patch.

Evidence after fix: Focused WhatsApp inbox and media tests passed, whitespace diff check passed, and autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Inbound monitor and auto-reply tests consume the nested message contexts for body, media, quote, group, event, platform reply, media send, and typing behavior, while fallback flat aliases remain populated.

What was not tested: Live WhatsApp device/session behavior and broad Testbox/CI lanes.

target.title: Keep agent web_search on runtime provider resolution
target.body:
Fixes #87347

## User-facing bug
Agent-side `web_search` could report `web_search is disabled or no provider is available` even when the Brave web search provider was already loaded in the runtime and worked through `openclaw infer web`. The agent tool was deciding that a manifest-owned configured provider should avoid runtime provider discovery before `runWebSearch` had a chance to use the active runtime registry.

## Exact repro
Source-level reproduction:

1. Configure `tools.web.search.provider` to a manifest-owned provider such as `brave`.
2. Start a runtime where the Brave web search provider is loaded and visible through runtime/plugin inspection.
3. Invoke the agent-side `web_search` tool with no active runtime metadata on the tool call.
4. Before this fix, `resolveWebSearchToolRuntimeContext()` consulted manifest ownership and passed `preferRuntimeProviders: false` to `runWebSearch`, so execution could diverge from the loaded runtime provider view.
5. After this fix, agent-side `web_search` keeps `preferRuntimeProviders: true`; `runWebSearch` still applies manifest ownership as a runtime load-scope hint, but it loads from the live runtime registry first.

## Fix summary
- Kept agent-side `web_search` runtime-provider discovery enabled regardless of configured provider manifest ownership.
- Left `web_fetch` behavior unchanged: bundled fetch owners still disable runtime provider discovery where that path expects static/bundled provider loading.
- Updated the late-bind and runtime-context tests so manifest-owned configured search providers now prove the runtime-first path instead of the old static-provider preference.

## Targeted tests
- Updated `src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.test.ts` to assert search never resolves manifest owners at the agent-tool preference gate, while fetch still does.
- Updated `src/agents/tools/web-search.late-bind.test.ts` to assert configured manifest-owned search providers pass `preferRuntimeProviders: true` into `runWebSearch`.
- Re-ran the existing `web_tools` defaults tests and focused `web-search/runtime` provider-loading tests to cover the downstream runtime behavior.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Agent-side `web_search` now uses the same live runtime provider path as `runWebSearch` / `openclaw infer web`, instead of switching configured manifest-owned providers to static provider loading at the agent-tool layer.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/agent-web-search-provider-resolution`, commit `1c171b7e`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `be127318`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.ts src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-search.late-bind.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-search.late-bind.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.enabled-defaults.test.ts src/web-search/runtime.test.ts -t "web tool runtime context|web_search late-bound runtime fallback|web tools defaults|scopes runtime provider loading|honors preferRuntimeProviders|keeps runtime provider loading"`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Targeted Vitest passed: `Test Files 7 passed (7)`, `Tests 40 passed | 23 skipped (63)`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed with no actionable regressions.

Observed result after fix: Manifest-owned configured search providers keep runtime discovery enabled at the agent tool boundary; the runtime path remains responsible for provider scoping.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` did not reach changed-file checks. In the sandbox it failed during `pnpm install` with npm registry DNS `ENOTFOUND`. An escalated retry got past DNS but still failed during `pnpm install` when Darwin optional package downloads timed out with error `23`.

## Not tested / known gaps
- No live Brave API request was run from this environment; validation is source-level with targeted runtime/tool tests.
- No live Brave-backed web search request was run from a real agent conversation; coverage is through provider-resolution tests.

## AI-assisted disclosure
This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used source-level reproduction, targeted regression tests, and `codex review --base origin/main` to validate the behavior above.

target.title: feat(feeds): add feed lifecycle tooling
target.body:
## Summary

Adds feed publisher/subscriber lifecycle tooling on top of the feed source and install primitives.

This PR adds:

- `openclaw feeds validate <path-or-url>` and `feeds hash <path-or-url>`.
- `openclaw feeds build --inventory <path> --out <path>` for deterministic curated feed artifacts.
- `openclaw feeds diff --previous <path> --current <path>` for publisher-side delta review.
- `openclaw feeds updates --installed <path>` to compare installed skill/plugin inventory against configured feeds.
- `openclaw feeds notices --installed <path>` for subscriber-facing update notices with install commands.
- Semver-aware update ordering, including prerelease vs stable comparisons.

## Example publisher flow

```bash
openclaw feeds validate ./company-feed.json --json
openclaw feeds hash ./company-feed.json
openclaw feeds build --inventory ./inventory.json --out ./company-feed.json --id company-approved
openclaw feeds diff --previous ./previous-feed.json --current ./company-feed.json --json
```

## Example subscriber flow

```bash
openclaw feeds updates --installed ./installed.json --approved-only --json
openclaw feeds notices --installed ./installed.json --approved-only --json
```

## Notes

This stays file-based and explicit. There is no daemon, background watch, auto-update, or ClawHub-native feed service in this PR. A later RFC/PR can add ClawHub-native feed support once the local feed contract is stable.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --noEmit
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md docs/plugins/reference.md
git diff --check
node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check
```

Focused stack proof on the final condensed branch: 243 tests passed.

## Feed PR stack

1. openclaw/openclaw#87824 - read-only feed discovery
2. openclaw/openclaw#87825 - approved feed installs
3. openclaw/openclaw#87826 - feed catalog policy conformance
4. openclaw/openclaw#87827 - feed lifecycle tooling
5. openclaw/openclaw#88732 - native feed search defaults and policy checks
6. openclaw/clawhub#2460 - ClawHub root feed lanes

The stack keeps OpenClaw as a feed consumer. ClawHub root feeds are producer infrastructure; enterprise or tenant feeds can be produced elsewhere using the same schema.

RFC draft: https://github.com/giodl73-repo/rfcs/blob/feeds-rfc-draft/rfcs/0004-feeds.md

target.title: Emit sessions.changed for in-chat command metadata
target.body:
Fixes #88598

## User-facing bug

Control UI clients subscribed to session updates could miss metadata changes made by in-chat commands. A command-only turn such as `/goal complete`, `/goal pause`, or `/goal clear` can update the session store without starting a model run, but the Gateway did not emit a `sessions.changed` event for that mutation. The visible session row could keep stale goal/status metadata until a manual reload or another unrelated session event.

## Exact repro

Source-level reproduction:

1. Connect a Control UI/WebChat client that subscribes to `sessions.changed`.
2. Send an in-chat `/goal` command that mutates the persisted session goal, for example `/goal complete shipped`.
3. The command handler writes the updated goal to the session store and returns command output.
4. Before this patch, no command-metadata `sessions.changed` event was broadcast for the store write, so subscribed clients did not get a scoped refresh for that session.

## Fix summary

- Add a typed `onSessionMetadataChanged` reply option for command handlers.
- Notify after successful command-session store writes through the shared `persistSessionEntry` helper.
- Notify `/goal` start, pause, resume, complete, blocked, clear, and status paths when they actually change the persisted goal metadata.
- Share the Gateway `sessions.changed` emitter between session RPC handlers and `chat.send`, so command-only chat turns can broadcast a scoped session row update.
- Delay command-metadata broadcasts until after the current command turn leaves the active-run map, while still preserving `hasActiveRun: true` if another run for the same session is active.
- Fall back to the shared session lifecycle event bus when command metadata changes happen outside `chat.send`, so raw channel dispatchers that use the same command-session store helper also refresh Gateway subscribers.
- Preserve selected-agent global alias handling so canonical `global` updates still refresh a selected `agent:<id>:global` Control UI/WebChat session.
- Include live session setting metadata (`thinkingLevels`, `thinkingOptions`, `thinkingDefault`) on command-metadata `sessions.changed` events, and reuse the TUI-loaded model catalog when rebuilding embedded session defaults.
- Document that command-only session metadata changes refresh subscribed clients.

## Targeted tests

- Added `/goal` command unit coverage for metadata-change notifications on mutating goal actions and no-op clear paths.
- Added `/goal` command unit coverage for the shared lifecycle fallback when no direct Gateway subscriber callback is installed.
- Added Gateway sessions coverage for command-metadata `sessions.changed` payloads and active-run exclusion.
- Added Gateway chat coverage proving command-only `/goal` turns broadcast scoped session updates and do not report their own command turn as active.
- Added Gateway lifecycle coverage proving command metadata lifecycle events are broadcast to session subscribers.
- Added UI controller coverage for canonical `global` deltas against selected agent-global aliases.
- Added embedded TUI coverage proving history model catalogs are reused for session metadata/defaults.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: In-chat command metadata writes now emit scoped `sessions.changed` events, so subscribed Control UI/WebChat clients can refresh visible session metadata after command-only turns.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/sessions-changed-in-chat-commands`, commit `ab4c78c7`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `be127318`. The Gateway proof starts a local WebSocket server/client pair and exercises the same `sessions.subscribe` plus `chat.send` event path used by Control UI/WebChat clients. The shared lifecycle fallback proof exercises command metadata notifications when no direct `chat.send` callback is unavailable.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- UI/TUI metadata proof passed: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 5 passed` for canonical global alias adoption and embedded TUI model catalog reuse. The combined command also attempted the Gateway metadata target, but default sandbox networking returned `listen EPERM 127.0.0.1`; I reran the Gateway target with elevated local-network permission.

It verifies mutating goal/directive commands call `onSessionMetadataChanged({ sessionKey, reason: "command-metadata" })`, no-op clear paths do not notify, and the shared lifecycle fallback emits `reason: "command-metadata"` when no direct subscriber callback is installed.

target.title: Render dashboard chat history incrementally
target.body:
Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87345

## User-facing bug
Switching dashboard chat sessions could synchronously rebuild the full 100-message UI history window, causing 8-10s switches on long or heavy transcripts.

## Exact repro
1. Open the Control UI chat dashboard.
2. Use or select a session whose `chat.history` returns the current 100-message tail.
3. Switch between dashboard sessions.
4. Before: the chat view can block while all loaded history items are built and rendered at once.
5. After: newly loaded large histories start with the latest 30-message render window, expand in 30-message batches when the user is at the top, avoid programmatic bottom-scroll expansion, and auto-fill non-scrollable initial windows while preserving bottom anchoring.

## Fix summary
- Added optional `historyRenderLimit` to `buildChatItems`, preserving existing default 100-message behavior.
- Added per-session render-window state in `renderChat` so newly loaded large histories initially render 30 messages.
- Expand the render window on top scroll or exact top state, skip bottom auto-scrolls inside the top threshold, and auto-fill after render when the initial window does not overflow.
- Keep auto-filled windows anchored to latest messages via the existing `onScrollToBottom` path.
- Search with a non-empty query still renders the full loaded and capped history window.

## Targeted tests

- `buildChatItems` coverage proves the render limit preserves the newest window.
- Chat view tests cover initial 30-message windows, top-scroll expansion, bottom-scroll suppression, auto-fill, and full rendering while searching.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Switching to a large dashboard chat history no longer forces an immediate 100-message render; the UI starts with a latest-message window and expands incrementally.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/dashboard-session-switching`, commit `16fc1330`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts -t "chat history render window"` (5 passed | 90 skipped)
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` (95 passed)
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed after resolving review findings.

Evidence after fix:
- Focused render-window Vitest passed: 5 passed, 90 skipped.
- Broader UI chat Vitest passed: 95 passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed after resolving review findings.

Observed result after fix: Newly loaded large histories render the newest 30 messages first, expand in 30-message batches from top-scroll/top state, avoid bottom auto-scroll expansion, and still render full loaded history for non-empty search.

What was not tested: `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` and `node scripts/check-changed.mjs` could not complete because both invoke `pnpm install`; sandbox got npm `ENOTFOUND`, and the escalated retry hit Darwin optional package download `error (23) The operation was aborted due to timeout`. Direct `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo` could not run because `node_modules/.bin/tsgo` was missing after the blocked install. No browser screenshot or video was captured; this PR changes render-window behavior and is covered with jsdom view tests, not visual layout changes.

## Not tested / known gaps
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` and `node scripts/check-changed.mjs` could not complete because both invoke `pnpm install`; sandbox got npm `ENOTFOUND`, and the escalated retry hit Darwin optional package download `error (23) The operation was aborted due to timeout`.
- Direct `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo` could not run because `node_modules/.bin/tsgo` was missing after the blocked install.

target.title: fix(gemini): bridge OAuth profiles into CLI runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- opt `google-gemini-cli` into explicit OpenClaw auth-profile forwarding
- refresh/load the selected OAuth profile before CLI backend preparation
- materialize selected Gemini OAuth credentials into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` for the `gemini` subprocess
- keep Gemini CLI auth epochs profile-owned when the bridge is active

Fixes #88742.

## Proof

HEAD: `d884722e235342d016211e82bc4085d228261259`

Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-validation.log`

Commands:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
pnpm -s exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Relevant output:

```text

Tests  59 passed (59)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Source-level behavior proof: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-proof.log`

- `extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts` proves selected OAuth credentials produce an isolated Gemini CLI home and writes `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` without logging token material.
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` proves canonical `google/*` models routed through `google-gemini-cli` select the `google-gemini-cli:*` auth profile before `runCliAgent`.

Not tested:

- Full `pnpm -s exec tsgo --noEmit` is blocked on current HEAD by pre-existing errors outside this patch:

```text
src/config/io.ts(192,7): error TS2527: The inferred type of 'configWritePostCommitRollback' references an inaccessible 'unique symbol' type. A type annotation is necessary.
src/secrets/config-io.ts(8,17): error TS4058: Return type of exported function has or is using name 'configWritePostCommitRollback' from external module ".../src/config/io" but cannot be named.
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `google-gemini-cli` backend preparation receives a selected OpenClaw `google-gemini-cli:*` OAuth profile, materializes that OAuth profile into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`, and passes that isolated home to the real `gemini` subprocess instead of relying on ambient `~/.gemini` state.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Linux workstation, PR branch `fix/88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge`, head `d884722e235342d016211e82bc4085d228261259`, OpenClaw `2026.5.31 (7150977)`, `@google/gemini-cli` binary available as `/usr/local/bin/gemini`, real Gemini CLI version `0.40.1`. Token values and account identifiers were not printed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
cd /home/rev/projects/openclaw-prs/pr-88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge
pnpm install
pnpm build
confirmed backend registration path:
  rg -n 'buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend|prepareExecution|CliBackendPlugin|cliBackends' src packages extensions/google | head -120
  nl -ba extensions/google/cli-backend.ts | sed -n '1,155p'
created direct backend proof harness:
  .tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts
ran:
  BAD_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy PROFILE='google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com' pnpm exec tsx ./.tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log
sanitized:
  sed -E -e 's/ya29\.[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-access-token>/g' -e 's#1//[A-Za-z0-9._-]+#<redacted-refresh-token>#g' -e 's/eyJ[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-jwt>/g' -e 's/AIza[[:alnum:]_-]+/<redacted-google-api-key>/g' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log > /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
verified no obvious token material remained:
  grep -E 'ya29\.|1//|eyJ|AIza' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
```

- **Evidence after fix**:


- **Observed result after fix**: `buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend().prepareExecution(...)` accepted a `google-gemini-cli` OAuth profile, created a private isolated Gemini CLI home, wrote `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` with Bearer OAuth credential shape and restrictive file permissions, returned a `GEMINI_CLI_HOME` different from the deliberately bad parent home, and a real `gemini` subprocess started successfully with that prepared environment. Cleanup removed the isolated home afterward.
- **What was not tested**: The proof intentionally did not print raw Google OAuth access tokens, refresh tokens, ID tokens, or private account data. The subprocess proof used `gemini --version` to prove the real Gemini CLI binary starts under the prepared `GEMINI_CLI_HOME`.
