target.title: Reject unpublished npm targets consistently in update dry-run
target.body:
Fixes #87320

## User-facing bug
`openclaw update --tag <version> --dry-run` could report a clean update plan when the requested OpenClaw version existed as a GitHub release but had not yet propagated to the npm registry. Running the same update without `--dry-run` then failed with npm `ETARGET`, so dry-run and apply gave contradictory signals during the npm propagation window.

## Exact repro
Source-level reproduction:

1. Use a package-manager install of OpenClaw.
2. Request an exact registry version target that is syntactically valid but absent from npm, for example:
   - `openclaw update --tag 2026.5.31 --dry-run --json`
3. Before this fix, the exact semver tag was accepted locally as `targetVersion: "2026.5.31"` without probing npm availability, so dry-run emitted a green plan.
4. Running apply with the same target later reached `npm install openclaw@2026.5.31` and failed with `ETARGET`/`E404`.
5. After this fix, dry-run and apply both fail early with a clear npm propagation message when the registry returns `HTTP 404` for the target.

## Fix summary
- Added an npm target availability preflight for package update targets that can be resolved through the npm registry.
- The preflight fails only when npm clearly reports the target as absent (`HTTP 404`), preserving existing behavior for transient network errors and non-registry package specs.
- The same preflight runs before both dry-run preview output and real package update install/stop-service work, so dry-run no longer green-lights a target apply will reject.
- The preflight now validates the actual resolved install spec, normalizes `v`-prefixed exact versions for registry lookup, and skips public-npm availability checks when `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_PACKAGE_SPEC` is explicitly authoritative.
- Registry probing now follows the actual resolved package manager path: npm global config for npm, pnpm config for pnpm, and no public npm availability preflight for Bun-managed installs.
- Public npm registry aliases are normalized, including HTTP and protocol-relative npmjs registry URLs, while custom registries keep Node runtime preflight coverage without forcing public npm target availability.

## Targeted tests
- Added dry-run coverage proving exact npm version targets absent from npm fail before JSON preview output.
- Added apply coverage proving the same absent target fails before `npm install` or managed service stop.
- Added coverage for `v`-prefixed exact versions and both tarball-style and registry-style package spec overrides.
- Added coverage for resolved global package managers, npm and pnpm registry config discovery, public npm registry aliases, custom registries, Bun-managed package updates, Node runtime preflight preservation, and semver-range rejection in registry npm specs.
- Re-ran focused update CLI and npm registry spec tests to catch package update regressions.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw update --tag <exact-version> --dry-run` and real apply now use the same npm target availability preflight, so dry-run does not approve a version npm will reject with `ETARGET`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/update-dry-run-npm-target`, commit `ad2f05b3`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `be127318`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `npm view openclaw@2026.5.31 version --json`
- `env OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1 node --import tsx -e "import { fetchNpmPackageTargetStatus } from './src/infra/update-check.ts'; const status = await fetchNpmPackageTargetStatus({ target: '2026.5.31', timeoutMs: 5000 }); console.log(JSON.stringify(status));"`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/infra/npm-registry-spec.test.ts -t "Bun-managed package updates|resolved global manager|pnpm registry config|protocol-relative npmjs registry|HTTP npmjs registry|explicit npm version target|v-prefixed exact npm targets|digit-starting npm dist-tags|custom registry|Node runtime preflight|custom global registry|lowercase npm registry env precedence|npm userconfig|semver range package specs|package spec overrides|accepts|rejects"`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `codex review --base origin/main`
- `OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof-87320 OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts update --channel stable --tag 2026.5.31 --dry-run --json --timeout 5`
- `OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof-87320 OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts update --channel stable --tag 2099.99.99 --dry-run --json --timeout 5`

Evidence after fix:
- Real npm registry lookup failed as expected after the latest rebase in the helper path: `npm error code E404`, `No match found for version 2026.5.31`, and `'openclaw@2026.5.31' is not in this registry.`
- The OpenClaw source npm target lookup helper returned `{"target":"2026.5.31","version":null,"nodeEngine":null,"error":"HTTP 404"}` after the latest rebase when network allowed that request.
- Focused Vitest passed after the latest rebase and review fixes: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 36 passed | 163 skipped (199)`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` was attempted and failed here due local app-server state write restrictions, not a code regression.
- Source-entry CLI reproduction commands returned parseable JSON payloads for both tag inputs:
  - `--tag 2026.5.31` returned `dryRun: true`, `tag: "openclaw@2026.5.31"`, and the action list.
  - `--tag 2099.99.99` returned the same structured JSON schema with `tag: "openclaw@2099.99.99"`.

Observed result after fix: Exact npm targets absent from the public npm registry fail before both JSON dry-run preview output and real package install/service stop work; explicit package spec overrides and Bun-managed installs keep their authoritative behavior.

What was not tested: The helper path `fetchNpmPackageTargetStatus` can return `TypeError: fetch failed` when local DNS/network restrictions block npm registry access; those failures are treated as non-blocking network errors by the preflight logic.

## Not tested / known gaps
- No live package-manager update was run against a real globally installed OpenClaw package; coverage is through a real npm registry lookup, the real OpenClaw npm target lookup helper, and update CLI regression tests with mocked package-manager calls.

## AI-assisted disclosure
This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used source-level issue reproduction, targeted regression tests, and explicit command output to validate the behavior above.

target.title: Preserve Mattermost streamed blocks in block mode
target.body:
Fixes #87322

## User-facing bug

Mattermost used the live draft-preview path even when the account opted into completed-block delivery. That meant logical block replies were edited into one draft preview instead of being delivered as stable visible block posts. In `streaming.mode: "block"`, finalizing the preview could also collapse a longer accumulated draft to only the last final segment.

## Exact repro

1. Configure Mattermost with preview streaming enabled and `blockStreaming: true`, or set `streaming.mode: "block"` and produce a final payload that is only the last streamed segment.
2. Send a prompt that produces multiple visible text blocks around tool/progress events.
3. Observe Mattermost editing/suppressing the block surface instead of preserving visible block delivery or a validated accumulated preview.

## Fix summary

- Added a Mattermost streaming delivery policy that keeps draft preview and block delivery mutually exclusive unless Mattermost explicitly chooses block delivery.
- Explicit Mattermost `blockStreaming: true` now disables draft preview and passes `disableBlockStreaming: false` to the reply pipeline.
- Active draft previews remain active even when `agents.defaults.blockStreamingDefault` is on unless Mattermost explicitly opts into block delivery.
- Partial-reply `delta`/`replace`/`phase` metadata now reaches channel preview callbacks, so Mattermost can distinguish accumulated final-answer transcript updates from stale progress text.
- Mattermost block-preview finalization can reuse accumulated preview text only when the partial stream proves it is an accumulated final-answer transcript, and the accumulated candidate passes the same chunk/limit validation as normal finals.
- Tool/progress suppression is now tied to an active draft preview so block delivery does not hide default progress messages.

## Targeted tests

- Mattermost monitor tests cover streaming policy selection, accumulated preview finalization, and draft-preview delivery.
- Auto-reply tests cover partial-reply metadata propagation through CLI assistant events and dispatch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Mattermost block-streaming configurations now preserve visible completed blocks instead of editing them into one draft preview or collapsing the final preview to only the last segment.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/mattermost-block-streaming-posts`, commit `7672dcdd`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts -t "resolveMattermostStreamingDeliveryPolicy|resolveMattermostPreviewFinalText|deliverMattermostReplyWithDraftPreview"`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -t "bridges CLI assistant agent events into onPartialReply|preserves embedded assistant partial metadata|bridges CLI assistant agent events into onReasoningStream"`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.test.ts`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Targeted Mattermost and auto-reply Vitest commands passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` found no blocking regression after the local fixes.

Observed result after fix: Mattermost only uses draft preview when the delivery policy selects preview mode, and explicit block streaming keeps completed blocks visible while progress suppression remains tied to active draft preview.

What was not tested: Live Mattermost server delivery was not run. `pnpm check:changed` is blocked by dependency install/network in this environment. `tsgo` typecheck is blocked because `node_modules/.bin/tsgo` is missing in this checkout.

## Not tested / known gaps

- Live Mattermost server delivery.
- `pnpm check:changed`, blocked by dependency install/network in this environment.
- `tsgo` typecheck, blocked because `node_modules/.bin/tsgo` is missing in this checkout.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This PR was prepared with AI assistance and local command/test verification.

target.title: perf(memory): batch memory embeddings across files
target.body:
## Summary

- Source-wide memory indexing now groups dirty files by provider batch request capacity instead of the old 4096 chunk guard.
- OpenAI and OpenAI-compatible batch polling now backs off from the configured interval, reports provider `request_counts` progress when available, and preserves day-long timeout configuration.
- Regression coverage now locks in split oversized-file chunks staying in one source-wide provider batch, OpenAI-compatible polling progress/backoff, and 1440-minute batch timeouts.
- Current-head type coverage also accepts per-agent subagent thinking defaults used by the existing ACP runtime test.

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88009

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw memory index --force --verbose` should batch a large mixed memory+session source set across file boundaries, including more than 4096 split chunks, instead of flushing early with `reason=max-chunks`; pending OpenAI batch status polling should back off and show provider progress instead of staying fixed at 2000ms with no progress counts.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw CLI from `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-88238-20260601`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, 833 memory markdown files, 495 session transcript files, provider `openai`, model `mistral/mistral-embed`, batch enabled, local OpenAI-compatible batch HTTP endpoint on `127.0.0.1:18184`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-88238-20260601
EVIDENCE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence PORT=18184 \
  node /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/prepare-maintainer-fixture.mjs
PORT=18184 STATS_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openai-compatible-batch-stats-maintainer.json \
  node /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openai-compatible-batch-server.mjs \
  > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/batch-server-maintainer.log 2>&1 &
server_pid=$!
OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/runtime-maintainer-home \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/runtime-maintainer-home/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
NO_COLOR=1 \
pnpm openclaw memory index --force --verbose \
  > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openclaw-memory-index-maintainer.log 2>&1
kill "$server_pid"
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[memory] embeddings: source-wide batch prepare files=1328 sources=memory=833,sessions=495 maxFiles=2048 maxRequests=50000
[memory] embeddings: source-wide batch submit group=1 source=memory+sessions files=1328 chunks=10228 sources=memory=833,sessions=495 reason=end
[memory] openai batch batch-1 in_progress; waiting 2000ms
[memory] openai batch batch-1 in_progress; progress 0/10228 failed=0; waiting 4000ms
[memory] openai batch batch-1 in_progress; progress 3409/10228 failed=0; waiting 8000ms
Memory index updated (main).
```

```json
{
  "uploads": [
    {
      "fileId": "file-1",
      "requests": 10228,
      "firstInputs": [
        "# Log\nMemory file 0 source-wide batch marker.\n",
        "# Log\nMemory file 1 source-wide batch marker.\n",
        "# Log\nMemory file 2 source-wide batch marker.\n"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "batches": [
    {
      "batchId": "batch-1",
      "inputFileId": "file-1",
      "outputFileId": "output-batch-1",
      "requests": 10228,
      "endpoint": "/v1/embeddings",
      "completionWindow": "24h"
    }
  ],
  "statusPolls": [
    { "batchId": "batch-1", "poll": 1 },
    { "batchId": "batch-1", "poll": 2 },
    { "batchId": "batch-1", "poll": 3 }
  ],
  "outputReads": [
    {
      "outputFileId": "output-batch-1",
      "responses": 10228
    }
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The maintainer-sized mixed source set produced one source-wide OpenAI batch upload with 10,228 embedding requests across all 1,328 files, and the source-wide submit reason was `end`, not the old chunk-limit split. The wait loop also backed off from 2000ms to 4000ms and 8000ms while surfacing provider request progress from `request_counts`.
- **What was not tested:** A live third-party embedding provider was not tested because this environment has no external embedding credentials; the real OpenClaw CLI and OpenAI-compatible batch HTTP contract were tested locally at the network boundary.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: real CLI behavior proof, focused runtime tests, source-wide memory integration test, lint, and type lanes.
- Target test file: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`; `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts`; `src/agents/memory-search.test.ts`; `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts`; `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-policy.test.ts`; `extensions/openai/memory-embedding-adapter.test.ts`; `extensions/openai/embedding-batch.test.ts`; `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-http.test.ts`; `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/post-json.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: source-wide batching keeps chunks split out of oversized files in the same provider batch job, forced memory+session indexing shares one source-wide batch, OpenAI-compatible polling reports progress and backs off while pending, 1440-minute timeout config is preserved, and the existing OpenAI batch adapter still sends document input type requests.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers the real source queue, provider runtime bridge, OpenAI/OpenAI-compatible batch transports, and the exact large mixed-source behavior the reporter re-tested.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts src/agents/memory-search.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 23.72s

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 69.44s

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-runner.test.ts extensions/openai/embedding-batch.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 33.25s

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-http.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/post-json.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-policy.test.ts extensions/openai/memory-embedding-adapter.test.ts extensions/openai/embedding-batch.test.ts src/agents/memory-search.test.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 103.45s

pnpm check:test-types
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
all completed with exit code 0

node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs
both completed with exit code 0

git diff --check
exit code 0
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The source-wide memory batching layer reused a 4096 chunk guard as the source-level flush limit, so large mixed-source runs could split before the provider batch layer reached its 50,000 request capacity. The wait logs also ignored provider batch `request_counts`, so slow providers could look stalled even when they reported progress.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered cross-file batching and provider batch submission, but they did not cover files that split into many chunks while still remaining below the provider batch request limit, visible polling progress/backoff for pending remote batch jobs, or preservation of day-long batch timeout config.

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex produced the implementation and PR shepherding with OpenAI `gpt-5.5`; ragesaq reviewed the direction and required production-grade proof before merge.

Pass a typed `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract through the context-engine lifecycle for embedded, CLI, and Codex app-server runs. Context engines now receive a versioned snapshot of runtime mode, model/provider state, host capabilities, token limits, and diagnostics instead of only the assemble-time `model` string.

## Changes

- Add `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` and `ContextEngineRuntimeMode` in `src/context-engine/types.ts`.
- Add `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Thread `runtimeSettings` through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, compact, and maintenance paths.
- Export the runtime-settings contract and errors through the public plugin SDK barrel.
- Preserve strict legacy context engines: if an engine rejects `runtimeSettings` as an unknown key, OpenClaw retries the lifecycle call without it instead of quarantining the engine.
- Document the public context-engine runtime-settings contract in `docs/concepts/context-engine.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`.
- Update the plugin SDK export contract test for the new public runtime-settings error exports.
- Add configured-slot proof that a resolved context engine receives runtimeSettings across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact.
- Preserve the same overflow compaction runtimeSettings into the immediately-following post-compaction maintenance call.

## Current Head

Current PR head: `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`.

Scope cleanup: the unrelated Copilot Gemini model-id commit `747f09e19610` has been removed from this PR by rewriting the branch over the current `release/2026.5.31` base. The current PR stack is 8 context-engine/runtime-settings commits, 20 files changed, and no `extensions/github-copilot` files in the diff.

## Review Findings Addressed

- [P1] Strict-schema legacy engines could quarantine on the new `runtimeSettings` key. Fixed by adding `runtimeSettings` to the registry legacy strip/retry set for bootstrap, maintain, afterTurn, assemble, and compact. Covered by focused compatibility tests. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P1] Current-head configured-plugin proof was missing. Added `passes runtime settings through a configured context engine across lifecycle hooks`, which registers an engine, resolves it through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, and verifies runtimeSettings on bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact. Commit: `9de0196a4b`.
- [P2] Overflow compaction built runtimeSettings for `compact()` but dropped them on immediate post-compaction maintenance. Fixed by reusing the same runtimeSettings object for both calls and covering the handoff in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`. Commit: `a767005b61`.
- [P2] `model.family` could mirror a concrete model id. Fixed by defaulting family to `null` unless a real family is supplied. Covered by regression tests. Commits: `2f1c92fc28`, `08ae320eca`.
- [P2] SDK contract was exported but not documented. Fixed by documenting `runtimeSettings` in context-engine docs and SDK overview. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P2] `compact()` documented `runtimeSettings`, but real compact callers did not pass it. Fixed embedded timeout/overflow compaction, queued/manual embedded compaction, CLI compaction, and follow-up maintenance to pass typed runtime settings. Commits: `b15732c179`, `a767005b61`.
- [P2] Production embedded paths did not populate requested/resolved/fallback/degraded fields. Fixed embedded attempts to capture requested model and pass fallback metadata through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, and maintenance. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- CI failure in `checks-fast-contracts-plugins-b`: fixed `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` to include `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnavailableError` and `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- Copilot review: `fallbackReason` and `fallbackActive` could be inconsistent. Fixed by deriving `fallbackActive` from any non-empty fallback reason. Covered by regression test. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- Copilot review: runtime-settings Error subclasses lacked names. Fixed. Commit: `2f1c92fc28`.
- Copilot review: missing builder test branches for degraded mode and invalid capabilities. Fixed. Commit: `14e1385788`.

## Current-Head Proof

Environment: OpenClaw source tree for this PR branch, run locally against current head `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`. Proof output is sanitized: no home paths, tokens, private endpoints, or raw transcripts are included here.

Commands run at current head:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm build
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1
```

Observed result:

```text
plugin-sdk-index.test.ts: 4 passed
context-engine.test.ts: 42 passed
runtime-settings.test.ts: 6 passed
context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts: 12 passed
run.overflow-compaction.test.ts: included in focused agent shard; focused agent shard 52 passed
pnpm check:test-types: exit 0
pnpm build: exit 0, including check-plugin-sdk-exports OK
git diff --check: clean
contracts-plugin config: 64 files, 894 tests passed
```

Configured context-engine proof added in `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- registers a test context engine with bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintain, and compact hooks
- resolves that engine through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`
- drives the resolved engine through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn/finalization maintenance, and compact timeout wrapper
- asserts every captured hook receives schema version 1 runtimeSettings
- asserts fallback mode, requested/resolved model split, provider, `family: null`, `fallbackActive: true`, and `fallbackReason`

Overflow compaction proof added in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`:

- drives overflow recovery through the embedded runner harness
- asserts the same runtimeSettings object reaches engine-owned overflow `compact()` and follow-up `runContextEngineMaintenance()`
- asserts post-compaction maintenance sees degraded mode and `degradedReason: "context_overflow"`

Compatibility proof added in `context-engine.test.ts`:

- strict legacy `assemble()` retries without `sessionKey`, `prompt`, and `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `bootstrap()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `maintain()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `afterTurn()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `compact()` retries without `runtimeSettings`

Runtime-settings proof added in `runtime-settings.test.ts` and `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- `model.family` defaults to `null` and never mirrors a concrete model id
- explicit `modelFamily` is preserved when supplied
- `fallbackReason` forces `fallbackActive: true` and `runtime.mode: "fallback"`
- `degradedReason` yields `runtime.mode: "degraded"`
- invalid host capabilities fail closed with `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`

## Compatibility and Risk

`runtimeSettings` remains optional on every lifecycle hook param. Engines that ignore it continue unchanged. Engines with strict unknown-field validation now get the same compatibility retry behavior OpenClaw already uses for legacy `sessionKey` and `prompt` additions.

Remaining review risk: current-head live Gateway proof is still a maintainer policy question. The branch now has current-head source-level configured-engine proof and current-head focused/full local validation, and it no longer contains the unrelated provider-default change.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts` -> pass, focused shards including 52 agent tests.
- `pnpm check:test-types` -> pass.
- `pnpm build` -> pass.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` -> pass, 894 tests total.

## Operator Understanding

ragesaq asked for proof from actual use before merge, called out that labels still required proof/triage proof, asked for PR follow-up and cleanup, then asked to clean up the unrelated Gemini commit. The PR has been rewritten onto the current release base so the public diff is now scoped to runtime settings only.

target.title: trace: Correlate channel message diagnostics into one trace
target.body:
Fixes #88811.

## Root Cause

Slack/channel message handling did not establish one diagnostic trace scope for the inbound message. The callback path can bypass the gateway request span, the embedded runner created a separate unrepresented diagnostic parent for model diagnostics, and diagnostics-otel only created `openclaw.message.processed` at completion time. That left harness/model diagnostics without a live message parent in OTel, queued async diagnostic ordering could make child spans arrive before the harness parent, and same-lifecycle file log records had no direct regression coverage for the channel trace scope.

## Implementation

- Add a per-inbound-channel-message diagnostic trace scope in the shared channel turn kernel.
- Run harness attempts inside a child diagnostic trace scope and let harness lifecycle diagnostics fall back to the active trace.
- Keep embedded model diagnostics under the active harness/message scope instead of minting an unrepresented intermediate parent.
- Start and track the `openclaw.message.processed` OTel span at message dispatch start for internal/trusted diagnostics, then finalize it after queued child diagnostics drain.
- Dispatch `harness.run.started` synchronously so it can anchor `openclaw.run`, `openclaw.model.usage`, and `openclaw.model.call` children.
- Add coverage for diagnostic event trace propagation, exported OTel waterfall relationships, production `logMessageDispatchStarted` / `logMessageProcessed` lifecycle helpers, and file log records emitted in the same channel lifecycle.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/agents/harness/v2.test.ts src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/agents/harness/v2.test.ts src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base origin/main` - no actionable correctness issues found

`node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed` did not reach project checks because local Crabbox failed its binary sanity check: `[crabbox] selected binary failed basic --version/--help sanity checks`.

Manual Tempo validation was not run locally. After deployment, validate with diagnostics-otel enabled and trace sampling at 1.0 by sending one Slack/channel message and confirming one Tempo trace waterfall contains `openclaw.message.processed`, `openclaw.harness.run`, `openclaw.model.usage`, and `openclaw.model.call` with one `traceId` and meaningful parent/child relationships.

AI-assisted change: implemented with Codex.

target.title: fix(reply): preserve sessions_send external routes
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88044.

- preserve a target session's persisted external delivery context for internal `sessions_send` handoffs that enter through the internal `webchat` surface
- carry persisted `deliveryContext.threadId` for inherited external routes so threaded transports stay on the stored thread
- mark inherited external routes in `resolveEffectiveReplyRoute` so dispatch can route them without making `sessions_send` look like a Feishu/Telegram/etc. user turn
- keep ordinary webchat turns on their live webchat route, and keep the stale-thread guard for non-inherited routes

## Behavior

Before this change, an agent-to-agent `sessions_send` turn could arrive as internal `webchat` and then fail to deliver a reply back to the existing Feishu session, even when the target session still had a valid external `deliveryContext`.

After this change, only `sessions_send` inter-session handoffs with a persisted deliverable external `deliveryContext` inherit that external route. If the persisted route has a `threadId`, dispatch passes it to `routeReply` only for that inherited route case. Normal Control UI/webchat turns continue to use their live webchat route, and ordinary routed replies still derive thread context from the active turn or thread-scoped session key.

## Verification

- `node --require ./node_modules/tsx/dist/preflight.cjs --import ./node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs .artifacts/proof/sessions-send-feishu-route-proof.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Focused threaded regression on `b024b9902b`: the route resolver now preserves `deliveryContext.threadId="thread:om_123"` for an inherited `sessions_send` external route, dispatch passes that same thread id to `routeReply`, and the existing stale-thread regression still proves ordinary/non-inherited routes do not resurrect a normalized stored thread id.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_send` replies can route through a target session's persisted external delivery context instead of being trapped as internal `webchat`; inherited external routes also preserve the persisted route thread when present.

Real environment tested: original issue #88044 reports a real OpenClaw 2026.5.27 Feishu/Lark setup with 7 agents, `tools.agentToAgent.enabled=true`, and `tools.sessions.visibility="all"`. The after-fix proof ran on patched branch `b024b9902b` using that same route shape: target session key `agent:B:feishu:B:direct:ou_xxx`, persisted `deliveryContext.channel="feishu"`, incoming `InputProvenance.kind="inter_session"`, `sourceTool="sessions_send"`, and live provider/surface/originating channel all `webchat`. To avoid live credentials in the PR, the proof registered a Feishu outbound adapter and exercised the production effective-route resolver plus durable external-channel delivery boundary.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --require ./node_modules/tsx/dist/preflight.cjs --import ./node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs .artifacts/proof/sessions-send-feishu-route-proof.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "inputProvenance": {
    "kind": "inter_session",
    "sourceTool": "sessions_send",
    "sourceChannel": "webchat"
  },
  "sessionKey": "agent:B:feishu:B:direct:ou_xxx",
  "persistedDeliveryContext": {
    "channel": "feishu",
    "to": "user:ou_123",
    "accountId": "work"
  },
  "liveTurnSurface": {
    "Provider": "webchat",
    "Surface": "webchat",
    "OriginatingChannel": "webchat",
    "OriginatingTo": "session:dashboard"
  },
  "effectiveRoute": {
    "channel": "feishu",
    "to": "user:ou_123",
    "accountId": "work",
    "inheritedExternalRoute": true
  },
  "durableSendResult": {
    "status": "sent",
    "results": [
      {
        "channel": "feishu",
        "messageId": "om_after_fix_proof_1",
        "chatId": "user:ou_123"
      }
    ]
  },
  "feishuSends": [
    {
      "adapter": "registered feishu outbound.sendText",
      "kind": "text",
      "to": "user:ou_123",
      "accountId": "work",
      "text": "QA-SESSIONS-SEND-FEISHU-PROOF",
      "replyToId": null,
      "threadId": null
    }
  ],
  "observed": "PASS: sessions_send webchat handoff used persisted Feishu deliveryContext"
}
```

Threaded regression evidence after fix: `src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts` covers inherited `sessions_send` external route resolution with `deliveryContext.threadId="thread:om_123"`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` verifies `routeReply` receives that inherited thread id. The same dispatch test file still passes the stale-thread guard case where ordinary routed replies must not read `deliveryContext.threadId` from normalized session store state.

Observed result after fix: the same route shape from #88044 now resolves the final reply route to `channel="feishu"`, `to="user:ou_123"`, `accountId="work"`, marks it `inheritedExternalRoute=true`, and the external-channel delivery boundary calls the registered Feishu outbound adapter with the proof marker. The focused regression suite also passed 198 tests and confirms normal webchat turns still stay on `webchat` while inherited threaded routes preserve `threadId`.

What was not tested: a fresh live Feishu tenant run against this patched branch was not rerun; the live Feishu evidence remains the original issue's redacted runtime logs, paired here with after-fix patched-branch external-channel delivery proof for the same persisted Feishu route shape and focused threaded route/dispatch regression coverage.

target.title: feat(policy): add feed catalog conformance
target.body:
## Summary

Adds Policy conformance checks for feed catalog posture.

This is config conformance, not runtime enforcement:

- Adds `policy.jsonc` feed rules for required feed source ids.
- Adds pinned-source and unsigned-source posture checks.
- Adds feed evidence to policy output only when feed policy rules are present.
- Redacts feed source URLs while preserving attestation drift detection with a stable evidence hash.
- Honors all feed activation gates before treating a configured feed source as enabled evidence.
- Adds policy docs, check ids, shape validation, and focused tests.

## Example syntax

```jsonc
{
  "feeds": {
    "sources": {
      "require": ["company-approved"],
      "requirePinned": true,
      "allowUnsigned": false
    }
  }
}
```

## Commands

```bash
openclaw policy check
openclaw policy check --json
openclaw doctor --lint
```

## Evidence shape

Feed evidence records the configured source id, config source path, enabled posture, trust mode, and whether valid pinned integrity is present. URLs are redacted to origin plus a short hash so policy attestation detects URL drift without exposing path, query, credentials, or local file paths.

## Notes

Policy reports findings when the active OpenClaw config does not conform to the approved `policy.jsonc`. It does not enforce install behavior; explicit feed install enforcement lives in the preceding feeds PR.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --noEmit
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md docs/plugins/reference.md
git diff --check
node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check
```

Focused stack proof on the final condensed branch: 243 tests passed.

## Feed PR stack

1. openclaw/openclaw#87824 - read-only feed discovery
2. openclaw/openclaw#87825 - approved feed installs
3. openclaw/openclaw#87826 - feed catalog policy conformance
4. openclaw/openclaw#87827 - feed lifecycle tooling
5. openclaw/openclaw#88732 - native feed search defaults and policy checks
6. openclaw/clawhub#2460 - ClawHub root feed lanes

The stack keeps OpenClaw as a feed consumer. ClawHub root feeds are producer infrastructure; enterprise or tenant feeds can be produced elsewhere using the same schema.

RFC draft: https://github.com/giodl73-repo/rfcs/blob/feeds-rfc-draft/rfcs/0004-feeds.md

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex produced the implementation and PR shepherding with OpenAI `gpt-5.5`; ragesaq reviewed the direction and required production-grade proof before merge.

Pass a typed `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract through the context-engine lifecycle for embedded, CLI, and Codex app-server runs. Context engines now receive a versioned snapshot of runtime mode, model/provider state, host capabilities, token limits, and diagnostics instead of only the assemble-time `model` string.

## Changes

- Add `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` and `ContextEngineRuntimeMode` in `src/context-engine/types.ts`.
- Add `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Thread `runtimeSettings` through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, compact, and maintenance paths.
- Export the runtime-settings contract and errors through the public plugin SDK barrel.
- Preserve strict legacy context engines: if an engine rejects `runtimeSettings` as an unknown key, OpenClaw retries the lifecycle call without it instead of quarantining the engine.
- Document the public context-engine runtime-settings contract in `docs/concepts/context-engine.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`.
- Update the plugin SDK export contract test for the new public runtime-settings error exports.
- Add configured-slot proof that a resolved context engine receives runtimeSettings across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact.
- Preserve the same overflow compaction runtimeSettings into the immediately-following post-compaction maintenance call.

## Current Head

Current PR head: `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`.

Scope cleanup: the unrelated Copilot Gemini model-id commit `747f09e19610` has been removed from this PR by rewriting the branch over the current `release/2026.5.31` base. The current PR stack is 8 context-engine/runtime-settings commits, 20 files changed, and no `extensions/github-copilot` files in the diff.

## Review Findings Addressed

- [P1] Strict-schema legacy engines could quarantine on the new `runtimeSettings` key. Fixed by adding `runtimeSettings` to the registry legacy strip/retry set for bootstrap, maintain, afterTurn, assemble, and compact. Covered by focused compatibility tests. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P1] Current-head configured-plugin proof was missing. Added `passes runtime settings through a configured context engine across lifecycle hooks`, which registers an engine, resolves it through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, and verifies runtimeSettings on bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact. Commit: `9de0196a4b`.
- [P2] Overflow compaction built runtimeSettings for `compact()` but dropped them on immediate post-compaction maintenance. Fixed by reusing the same runtimeSettings object for both calls and covering the handoff in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`. Commit: `a767005b61`.
- [P2] `model.family` could mirror a concrete model id. Fixed by defaulting family to `null` unless a real family is supplied. Covered by regression tests. Commits: `2f1c92fc28`, `08ae320eca`.
- [P2] SDK contract was exported but not documented. Fixed by documenting `runtimeSettings` in context-engine docs and SDK overview. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P2] `compact()` documented `runtimeSettings`, but real compact callers did not pass it. Fixed embedded timeout/overflow compaction, queued/manual embedded compaction, CLI compaction, and follow-up maintenance to pass typed runtime settings. Commits: `b15732c179`, `a767005b61`.
- [P2] Production embedded paths did not populate requested/resolved/fallback/degraded fields. Fixed embedded attempts to capture requested model and pass fallback metadata through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, and maintenance. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- CI failure in `checks-fast-contracts-plugins-b`: fixed `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` to include `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnavailableError` and `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- Copilot review: `fallbackReason` and `fallbackActive` could be inconsistent. Fixed by deriving `fallbackActive` from any non-empty fallback reason. Covered by regression test. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- Copilot review: runtime-settings Error subclasses lacked names. Fixed. Commit: `2f1c92fc28`.
- Copilot review: missing builder test branches for degraded mode and invalid capabilities. Fixed. Commit: `14e1385788`.

## Current-Head Proof

Environment: OpenClaw source tree for this PR branch, run locally against current head `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`. Proof output is sanitized: no home paths, tokens, private endpoints, or raw transcripts are included here.

Commands run at current head:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm build
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1
```

Observed result:

```text
plugin-sdk-index.test.ts: 4 passed
context-engine.test.ts: 42 passed
runtime-settings.test.ts: 6 passed
context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts: 12 passed
run.overflow-compaction.test.ts: included in focused agent shard; focused agent shard 52 passed
pnpm check:test-types: exit 0
pnpm build: exit 0, including check-plugin-sdk-exports OK
git diff --check: clean
contracts-plugin config: 64 files, 894 tests passed
```

Configured context-engine proof added in `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- registers a test context engine with bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintain, and compact hooks
- resolves that engine through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`
- drives the resolved engine through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn/finalization maintenance, and compact timeout wrapper
- asserts every captured hook receives schema version 1 runtimeSettings
- asserts fallback mode, requested/resolved model split, provider, `family: null`, `fallbackActive: true`, and `fallbackReason`

Overflow compaction proof added in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`:

- drives overflow recovery through the embedded runner harness
- asserts the same runtimeSettings object reaches engine-owned overflow `compact()` and follow-up `runContextEngineMaintenance()`
- asserts post-compaction maintenance sees degraded mode and `degradedReason: "context_overflow"`

Compatibility proof added in `context-engine.test.ts`:

- strict legacy `assemble()` retries without `sessionKey`, `prompt`, and `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `bootstrap()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `maintain()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `afterTurn()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `compact()` retries without `runtimeSettings`

Runtime-settings proof added in `runtime-settings.test.ts` and `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- `model.family` defaults to `null` and never mirrors a concrete model id
- explicit `modelFamily` is preserved when supplied
- `fallbackReason` forces `fallbackActive: true` and `runtime.mode: "fallback"`
- `degradedReason` yields `runtime.mode: "degraded"`
- invalid host capabilities fail closed with `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`

## Compatibility and Risk

`runtimeSettings` remains optional on every lifecycle hook param. Engines that ignore it continue unchanged. Engines with strict unknown-field validation now get the same compatibility retry behavior OpenClaw already uses for legacy `sessionKey` and `prompt` additions.

Remaining review risk: current-head live Gateway proof is still a maintainer policy question. The branch now has current-head source-level configured-engine proof and current-head focused/full local validation, and it no longer contains the unrelated provider-default change.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts` -> pass, focused shards including 52 agent tests.
- `pnpm check:test-types` -> pass.
- `pnpm build` -> pass.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` -> pass, 894 tests total.

## Operator Understanding

ragesaq asked for proof from actual use before merge, called out that labels still required proof/triage proof, asked for PR follow-up and cleanup, then asked to clean up the unrelated Gemini commit. The PR has been rewritten onto the current release base so the public diff is now scoped to runtime settings only.

target.title: fix(telegram): slow polling restart storms
target.body:
## Summary
- Increase Telegram polling restart backoff from the tight 2s/30s policy to a bounded 30s/10m exponential policy.
- Deduplicate runner/bot stop calls per polling restart cycle so watchdog restarts do not stack repeated stop requests.
- Add stop-timeout burst cooldown and reset restart state after healthy normal and isolated-ingress polling cycles, including isolated forced-restart stop timeouts.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** Consecutive Telegram polling stop timeouts no longer restart on the same short 30s ceiling. The second stop-timeout burst applies a separate cooldown, while a healthy normal or isolated polling cycle resets restart attempts back to the first-delay path.
- **Real environment tested:** Isolated PR-head worktree `/private/tmp/openclaw-pr88832-proof` at `0579b14240079e4c791646a5b09e4bf39f8bff15`, running the actual `TelegramPollingSession` isolated-ingress restart loop, actual wall-clock timers, actual restart/backoff helper, and a real grammY `Bot` instance pointed at a refused local Telegram API endpoint (`127.0.0.1:9`) with a redacted dummy token. The ingress worker was production-like but local: it emitted a real `poll-start`, held the in-flight getUpdates open, and resolved `stop()` only after the 15s stop grace so the session exercised the forced stop-timeout path without using production credentials.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the stop-timeout/network-flap proof below through `node --import tsx proof-telegram-stop-timeout.mjs`, ran the helper proof below through `node --import tsx`, then ran focused Vitest cases against `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` that exercise normal restart backoff, isolated `poll-success` reset, and two consecutive isolated forced stop-timeout restarts reaching the cooldown policy.
- **Evidence after fix:** Helper output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
firstStopTimeoutDelayMs=33000
firstStopTimeoutSuffix=""
secondStopTimeoutDelayMs=132000
secondStopTimeoutSuffix=" Stop timeout burst=2; applying cooldown."
afterHealthyDelayMs=33000
afterHealthySuffix=""
```

- **Production-like stop-timeout/network-flap proof from PR head:**

```text
$ node --import tsx proof-telegram-stop-timeout.mjs

+30.1s [telegram] Polling stall detected (active getUpdates stuck for 60.98s); forcing restart. [diag inFlight=1 outcome=started startedAt=<redacted> finishedAt=n/a durationMs=n/a offset=n/a]
+45.1s [telegram] Isolated polling ingress stop timed out after 15s; forcing restart cycle.
+45.1s Telegram isolated polling ingress restart requested; restarting in 33.53s.
+108.7s [telegram] Polling stall detected (active getUpdates stuck for 60.98s); forcing restart. [diag inFlight=1 outcome=started startedAt=<redacted> finishedAt=n/a durationMs=n/a offset=n/a]
+123.7s [telegram] Isolated polling ingress stop timed out after 15s; forcing restart cycle.
+123.7s Telegram isolated polling ingress restart requested; restarting in 124.2s. Stop timeout burst=2; applying cooldown.
PROOF_SUMMARY_START
head=0579b14240079e4c791646a5b09e4bf39f8bff15
workerCycles=2
containsStopTimeoutCooldown=true
elapsedMs=124702
PROOF_SUMMARY_END
```

- **Production-like isolated polling focused test proof:**

```text
$ node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "drains Telegram delivery queue after isolated ingress reports poll success|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|uses backoff helpers|resets restart backoff|cools down repeated|forces a restart when polling stalls without getUpdates activity|forces a restart when the runner task is pending" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000

 RUN  v4.1.7 /private/tmp/openclaw-pr88832-proof

··----··-·-------------------------···---------------

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed | 45 skipped (53)
   Start at  18:42:52
   Duration  6.63s (transform 5.09s, setup 193ms, import 166ms, tests 6.19s, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first stop timeout uses the normal 30s-class restart policy, the second consecutive stop timeout is raised by the stop-timeout cooldown path, the normal healthy cycle resets to the first-attempt delay, isolated-ingress `poll-success` resets the real session backoff state, and isolated forced stop-timeout restarts now wait through the same cooldown/backoff path instead of immediately continuing.
- **What was not tested:** No production Telegram bot token was used and no message was sent. The production-like proof intentionally used a local refused Telegram API endpoint and a redacted dummy token to avoid credential exposure or disturbing the running gateway. The full local `polling-session.test.ts` file also hits an existing `upstream/main` hang in `keeps active spooled lanes blocked across isolated ingress restarts`; this was reproduced in a detached `upstream/main` worktree, so verification used focused polling-session cases plus `monitor.test`.

## Verification
- `node --import tsx proof-telegram-stop-timeout.mjs`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "drains Telegram delivery queue after isolated ingress reports poll success|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|uses backoff helpers|resets restart backoff|cools down repeated|forces a restart when polling stalls without getUpdates activity|forces a restart when the runner task is pending" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|restarts isolated ingress when worker liveness stalls|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|cools down repeated" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/monitor.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`

target.title: feat(dreaming): score candidates with shadow trial results
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a small report-only scoring layer for dreaming shadow-trial results.

This follows the path from #83719: the shadow trial can now produce a candidate scoring view, but it still does not write to `MEMORY.md` or apply promotion by itself.

The helper keeps the policy simple:

- `helpful` gets a small bounded score boost
- `neutral` stays deferred without a boost
- `harmful` is rejected for that scoring pass

This gives the next dreaming step something useful to rank and review without changing the durable memory write path.

The scoring helpers are intentionally kept internal for now. The public `memory-core/api.ts` surface remains limited to the report-only shadow-trial artifact contract from #83719 until a real report integration proves the permanent scoring consumer.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Dreaming shadow-trial results should be usable as a bounded review signal for candidate ranking while preserving the report-only contract from #83719. A harmful trial should reject a candidate for the scoring pass, and none of this should mutate durable memory.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, built from current `upstream/main` after #83719. The focused tests use the memory-core unit harness and temporary workspaces with real files on disk. I also ran a direct memory-core invocation against a temporary workspace with a real `MEMORY.md` file.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
git diff --check
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts extensions/memory-core/api.ts docs/concepts/dreaming.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/concepts/dreaming.md
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts
pnpm lint --threads=8
node --import tsx -e "import('./extensions/memory-core/api.ts').then((m)=>{ if (typeof m.buildDreamingShadowTrialReport !== 'function' || typeof m.writeDreamingShadowTrialReport !== 'function') process.exit(1); if ('scoreDreamingShadowTrialCandidate' in m || 'rankDreamingShadowTrialCandidates' in m) process.exit(2); console.log('api exports scoped ok'); })"
node --import tsx --input-type=module
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts: 11 passed
dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts + short-term-promotion.test.ts: 77 passed
dreaming.test.ts + dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts: 59 passed
Docs MDX check passed (1 files)
All matched files use the correct format.
api exports scoped ok
pnpm lint --threads=8 passed
```

Direct memory-core invocation output:

```json
{
  "helpful": {
    "scoreBeforeShadowTrial": 0.72,
    "shadowTrialScoreDelta": 0.04,
    "scoreAfterShadowTrial": 0.76,
    "scoringAction": "report-only",
    "rejectedByShadowTrial": false
  },
  "harmful": {
    "scoreAfterShadowTrial": 0,
    "rejectedByShadowTrial": true
  },
  "rankingOrder": [
    "candidate-helpful",
    "candidate-harmful"
  ],
  "reportPath": "memory/dreaming/shadow-trials/2026-06-01/e3707886e038.md",
  "promotionAction": "report-only",
  "memoryUnchanged": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: Helpful shadow-trial results raise the score by a bounded amount, neutral results leave the score unchanged, harmful results set the scoring result to rejected with score `0`, and rejected candidates sort behind non-rejected candidates by candidate id rather than object identity. Missing evidence remains empty machine data while markdown still renders a human placeholder. The direct invocation wrote a report-only shadow-trial artifact, ranked the helpful candidate ahead of the harmful candidate, kept `promotionAction` as `report-only`, and read back the temporary `MEMORY.md` unchanged.

What was not tested: I did not wire this into automatic deep-phase promotion or any `MEMORY.md` write path. `pnpm tsgo:extensions` currently fails in unrelated `extensions/github-copilot/index.ts` code outside this PR's diff, so I used focused memory-core tests plus lint/docs/format/export checks for this change.

## Tests

- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts extensions/memory-core/api.ts docs/concepts/dreaming.md`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/concepts/dreaming.md`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts`

If this gets landed manually, please keep the original commit author attribution.

target.title: fix(telegram): slow polling restart storms
target.body:
## Summary
- Increase Telegram polling restart backoff from the tight 2s/30s policy to a bounded 30s/10m exponential policy.
- Deduplicate runner/bot stop calls per polling restart cycle so watchdog restarts do not stack repeated stop requests.
- Add stop-timeout burst cooldown and reset restart state after healthy normal and isolated-ingress polling cycles, including isolated forced-restart stop timeouts.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** Consecutive Telegram polling stop timeouts no longer restart on the same short 30s ceiling. The second stop-timeout burst applies a separate cooldown, while a healthy normal or isolated polling cycle resets restart attempts back to the first-delay path.
- **Real environment tested:** Isolated PR-head worktree `/private/tmp/openclaw-pr88832-proof` at `0579b14240079e4c791646a5b09e4bf39f8bff15`, running the actual `TelegramPollingSession` isolated-ingress restart loop, actual wall-clock timers, actual restart/backoff helper, and a real grammY `Bot` instance pointed at a refused local Telegram API endpoint (`127.0.0.1:9`) with a redacted dummy token. The ingress worker was production-like but local: it emitted a real `poll-start`, held the in-flight getUpdates open, and resolved `stop()` only after the 15s stop grace so the session exercised the forced stop-timeout path without using production credentials.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the stop-timeout/network-flap proof below through `node --import tsx proof-telegram-stop-timeout.mjs`, ran the helper proof below through `node --import tsx`, then ran focused Vitest cases against `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` that exercise normal restart backoff, isolated `poll-success` reset, and two consecutive isolated forced stop-timeout restarts reaching the cooldown policy.
- **Evidence after fix:** Helper output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
firstStopTimeoutDelayMs=33000
firstStopTimeoutSuffix=""
secondStopTimeoutDelayMs=132000
secondStopTimeoutSuffix=" Stop timeout burst=2; applying cooldown."
afterHealthyDelayMs=33000
afterHealthySuffix=""
```

- **Production-like stop-timeout/network-flap proof from PR head:**

```text
$ node --import tsx proof-telegram-stop-timeout.mjs

+30.1s [telegram] Polling stall detected (active getUpdates stuck for 60.98s); forcing restart. [diag inFlight=1 outcome=started startedAt=<redacted> finishedAt=n/a durationMs=n/a offset=n/a]
+45.1s [telegram] Isolated polling ingress stop timed out after 15s; forcing restart cycle.
+45.1s Telegram isolated polling ingress restart requested; restarting in 33.53s.
+108.7s [telegram] Polling stall detected (active getUpdates stuck for 60.98s); forcing restart. [diag inFlight=1 outcome=started startedAt=<redacted> finishedAt=n/a durationMs=n/a offset=n/a]
+123.7s [telegram] Isolated polling ingress stop timed out after 15s; forcing restart cycle.
+123.7s Telegram isolated polling ingress restart requested; restarting in 124.2s. Stop timeout burst=2; applying cooldown.
PROOF_SUMMARY_START
head=0579b14240079e4c791646a5b09e4bf39f8bff15
workerCycles=2
containsStopTimeoutCooldown=true
elapsedMs=124702
PROOF_SUMMARY_END
```

- **Production-like isolated polling focused test proof:**

```text
$ node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "drains Telegram delivery queue after isolated ingress reports poll success|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|uses backoff helpers|resets restart backoff|cools down repeated|forces a restart when polling stalls without getUpdates activity|forces a restart when the runner task is pending" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000

 RUN  v4.1.7 /private/tmp/openclaw-pr88832-proof

··----··-·-------------------------···---------------

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed | 45 skipped (53)
   Start at  18:42:52
   Duration  6.63s (transform 5.09s, setup 193ms, import 166ms, tests 6.19s, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first stop timeout uses the normal 30s-class restart policy, the second consecutive stop timeout is raised by the stop-timeout cooldown path, the normal healthy cycle resets to the first-attempt delay, isolated-ingress `poll-success` resets the real session backoff state, and isolated forced stop-timeout restarts now wait through the same cooldown/backoff path instead of immediately continuing.
- **What was not tested:** No production Telegram bot token was used and no message was sent. The production-like proof intentionally used a local refused Telegram API endpoint and a redacted dummy token to avoid credential exposure or disturbing the running gateway. The full local `polling-session.test.ts` file also hits an existing `upstream/main` hang in `keeps active spooled lanes blocked across isolated ingress restarts`; this was reproduced in a detached `upstream/main` worktree, so verification used focused polling-session cases plus `monitor.test`.

## Verification
- `node --import tsx proof-telegram-stop-timeout.mjs`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "drains Telegram delivery queue after isolated ingress reports poll success|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|uses backoff helpers|resets restart backoff|cools down repeated|forces a restart when polling stalls without getUpdates activity|forces a restart when the runner task is pending" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|restarts isolated ingress when worker liveness stalls|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|cools down repeated" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/monitor.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`

Refs #88256

commands.author: ragesaq

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex produced the implementation and PR shepherding with OpenAI `gpt-5.5`; ragesaq reviewed the direction and required production-grade proof before merge.

Pass a typed `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract through the context-engine lifecycle for embedded, CLI, and Codex app-server runs. Context engines now receive a versioned snapshot of runtime mode, model/provider state, host capabilities, token limits, and diagnostics instead of only the assemble-time `model` string.

## Changes

- Add `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` and `ContextEngineRuntimeMode` in `src/context-engine/types.ts`.
- Add `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Thread `runtimeSettings` through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, compact, and maintenance paths.
- Export the runtime-settings contract and errors through the public plugin SDK barrel.
- Preserve strict legacy context engines: if an engine rejects `runtimeSettings` as an unknown key, OpenClaw retries the lifecycle call without it instead of quarantining the engine.
- Document the public context-engine runtime-settings contract in `docs/concepts/context-engine.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`.
- Update the plugin SDK export contract test for the new public runtime-settings error exports.
- Add configured-slot proof that a resolved context engine receives runtimeSettings across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact.
- Preserve the same overflow compaction runtimeSettings into the immediately-following post-compaction maintenance call.

## Current Head

Current PR head: `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`.

Scope cleanup: the unrelated Copilot Gemini model-id commit `747f09e19610` has been removed from this PR by rewriting the branch over the current `release/2026.5.31` base. The current PR stack is 8 context-engine/runtime-settings commits, 20 files changed, and no `extensions/github-copilot` files in the diff.

## Review Findings Addressed

- [P1] Strict-schema legacy engines could quarantine on the new `runtimeSettings` key. Fixed by adding `runtimeSettings` to the registry legacy strip/retry set for bootstrap, maintain, afterTurn, assemble, and compact. Covered by focused compatibility tests. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P1] Current-head configured-plugin proof was missing. Added `passes runtime settings through a configured context engine across lifecycle hooks`, which registers an engine, resolves it through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, and verifies runtimeSettings on bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact. Commit: `9de0196a4b`.
- [P2] Overflow compaction built runtimeSettings for `compact()` but dropped them on immediate post-compaction maintenance. Fixed by reusing the same runtimeSettings object for both calls and covering the handoff in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`. Commit: `a767005b61`.
- [P2] `model.family` could mirror a concrete model id. Fixed by defaulting family to `null` unless a real family is supplied. Covered by regression tests. Commits: `2f1c92fc28`, `08ae320eca`.
- [P2] SDK contract was exported but not documented. Fixed by documenting `runtimeSettings` in context-engine docs and SDK overview. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P2] `compact()` documented `runtimeSettings`, but real compact callers did not pass it. Fixed embedded timeout/overflow compaction, queued/manual embedded compaction, CLI compaction, and follow-up maintenance to pass typed runtime settings. Commits: `b15732c179`, `a767005b61`.
- [P2] Production embedded paths did not populate requested/resolved/fallback/degraded fields. Fixed embedded attempts to capture requested model and pass fallback metadata through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, and maintenance. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- CI failure in `checks-fast-contracts-plugins-b`: fixed `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` to include `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnavailableError` and `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- Copilot review: `fallbackReason` and `fallbackActive` could be inconsistent. Fixed by deriving `fallbackActive` from any non-empty fallback reason. Covered by regression test. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- Copilot review: runtime-settings Error subclasses lacked names. Fixed. Commit: `2f1c92fc28`.
- Copilot review: missing builder test branches for degraded mode and invalid capabilities. Fixed. Commit: `14e1385788`.

## Current-Head Proof

Environment: OpenClaw source tree for this PR branch, run locally against current head `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`. Proof output is sanitized: no home paths, tokens, private endpoints, or raw transcripts are included here.

Commands run at current head:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm build
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1
```

Observed result:

```text
plugin-sdk-index.test.ts: 4 passed
context-engine.test.ts: 42 passed
runtime-settings.test.ts: 6 passed
context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts: 12 passed
run.overflow-compaction.test.ts: included in focused agent shard; focused agent shard 52 passed
pnpm check:test-types: exit 0
pnpm build: exit 0, including check-plugin-sdk-exports OK
git diff --check: clean
contracts-plugin config: 64 files, 894 tests passed
```

Configured context-engine proof added in `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- registers a test context engine with bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintain, and compact hooks
- resolves that engine through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`
- drives the resolved engine through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn/finalization maintenance, and compact timeout wrapper
- asserts every captured hook receives schema version 1 runtimeSettings
- asserts fallback mode, requested/resolved model split, provider, `family: null`, `fallbackActive: true`, and `fallbackReason`

Overflow compaction proof added in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`:

- drives overflow recovery through the embedded runner harness
- asserts the same runtimeSettings object reaches engine-owned overflow `compact()` and follow-up `runContextEngineMaintenance()`
- asserts post-compaction maintenance sees degraded mode and `degradedReason: "context_overflow"`

Compatibility proof added in `context-engine.test.ts`:

- strict legacy `assemble()` retries without `sessionKey`, `prompt`, and `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `bootstrap()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `maintain()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `afterTurn()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `compact()` retries without `runtimeSettings`

Runtime-settings proof added in `runtime-settings.test.ts` and `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- `model.family` defaults to `null` and never mirrors a concrete model id
- explicit `modelFamily` is preserved when supplied
- `fallbackReason` forces `fallbackActive: true` and `runtime.mode: "fallback"`
- `degradedReason` yields `runtime.mode: "degraded"`
- invalid host capabilities fail closed with `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`

## Compatibility and Risk

`runtimeSettings` remains optional on every lifecycle hook param. Engines that ignore it continue unchanged. Engines with strict unknown-field validation now get the same compatibility retry behavior OpenClaw already uses for legacy `sessionKey` and `prompt` additions.

Remaining review risk: current-head live Gateway proof is still a maintainer policy question. The branch now has current-head source-level configured-engine proof and current-head focused/full local validation, and it no longer contains the unrelated provider-default change.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts` -> pass, focused shards including 52 agent tests.
- `pnpm check:test-types` -> pass.
- `pnpm build` -> pass.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` -> pass, 894 tests total.

## Operator Understanding

ragesaq asked for proof from actual use before merge, called out that labels still required proof/triage proof, asked for PR follow-up and cleanup, then asked to clean up the unrelated Gemini commit. The PR has been rewritten onto the current release base so the public diff is now scoped to runtime settings only.

## Summary

- Fix unrelated `check-lint` failure from PR #88847 by satisfying new extension oxlint rules in `extensions/microsoft-foundry/provider.ts`.
- Replace redundant boolean literal comparisons with direct boolean expressions.
- Remove unnecessary `{}` fallbacks from object spreads.
